message:
feat(rch): add config doctor and config edit commands

Implement two new configuration management commands to improve the
operator experience:

## config doctor
Comprehensive health diagnostics that go beyond syntax validation:
- Socket path writability check (can daemon start?)
- SSH identity file existence and permission validation (600/400)
- Glob pattern syntax verification for exclude patterns
- File permission warnings for world-readable config directories
- Daemon connectivity status check

Outputs structured diagnostics with severity levels (error/warning/info),
unique codes (DOC-Exxx, DOC-Wxxx, DOC-Ixxx), and actionable remediation
suggestions. Supports --json for CI integration with exit code 1 on errors.

## config edit
Opens configuration files in $EDITOR/$VISUAL (falls back to nano):
- --project: Edit .rch/config.toml in current directory
- --user: Edit ~/.config/rch/config.toml (default)
- --workers: Edit ~/.config/rch/workers.toml

Creates files with helpful header comments if they don't exist.
Validates configuration after saving and reports any issues.

Adds glob crate dependency for pattern validation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
